name: Wat Chocolate Factory
khmerName: រោងចក្រសូកូឡា កាកាវ
description: Cambodia's first and only bean-to-bar chocolate factory, sourcing cacao directly from local Cambodian farmers.
uniqueFact: Cambodia grows some of Southeast Asia's finest cacao, and this factory was the first to process it entirely on Cambodian soil.
highlights: Guided tour of the full chocolate-making process from bean to bar
Taste test of single-origin Cambodian chocolate
Watch tempering and molding in action
Purchase unique souvenirs and gift boxes
tip: Book a tour in advance — group sizes are small and slots fill quickly.

name: Tonle Sap Floating Village
khmerName: ភូមិអណ្តែតទន្លេសាប
description: Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake hosts entire floating villages that dramatically shift between wet and dry seasons.
uniqueFact: The lake expands from 2,500 km² in the dry season to over 16,000 km² in the wet season — entire communities move with it.
highlights: Boat tour through the floating village
Visit the floating school and health clinic
Watch fishermen haul in their catch
Stunning sunset views over the water
tip: Visit between October and December when water levels are high and the village is most impressive.

name: Angkor National Museum
khmerName: សារមន្ទីរជាតិអង្គរ
description: A world-class museum dedicated to the Khmer civilization and the Angkor period, with over 1,000 artifacts across eight themed galleries.
uniqueFact: The Gallery of a Thousand Buddhas houses one of the largest collections of Buddha statues in Southeast Asia.
highlights: Gallery of a Thousand Buddhas
Multimedia presentations on Khmer history
Detailed scale models of Angkor Wat
Artifacts spanning over 1,000 years of Khmer civilization
tip: Visit before touring the temples — the context makes the ruins far more meaningful.

name: Cambodia Landmine Museum
khmerName: សារមន្ទីរមីនកម្ពុជា
description: Founded by former child soldier and deminer Aki Ra, this museum documents Cambodia's tragic landmine history and ongoing demining efforts.
uniqueFact: Aki Ra personally defused thousands of landmines by hand using basic tools before founding the museum and the Cambodian Self Help Demining organization.
highlights: Displays of defused mines and unexploded ordnance
Aki Ra's personal story and demining journey
Information on ongoing demining efforts across Cambodia
Proceeds support the relief center for landmine victims
tip: Combine with a visit to the War Museum nearby. Allow time — this is a deeply moving experience.

name: The Muffin Man Café
khmerName: ហាងកាហ្វេ មាហ្វីន ម៉ែន
description: A beloved bakery café famous across Siem Reap for its extraordinary rotating selection of Basque cheesecakes in flavors you won't find anywhere else.
uniqueFact: The Basque cheesecake here has developed a cult following among expats and travelers — flavors rotate daily and sell out fast.
highlights: Rotating daily Basque cheesecake flavors
Fresh baked pastries and artisan breads
Excellent coffee in a relaxed garden setting
Popular with locals and expats alike
tip: Arrive early or pre-order your cheesecake — popular flavors are gone by mid-morning.

name: Spoons Restaurant
khmerName: ភោជនីយដ្ឋាន ស្ពូន
description: A social enterprise restaurant staffed entirely by disadvantaged Cambodian youth who are trained in hospitality and culinary arts.
uniqueFact: Every meal you eat directly funds vocational training and education for at-risk Cambodian youth, giving them a path out of poverty.
highlights: Authentic Khmer cuisine prepared by students
Watch trainees develop real hospitality skills
Rotating seasonal menu featuring local ingredients
Meaningful dining experience that directly supports the community
tip: Make a reservation — it fills up quickly, especially in peak season.

name: Phnom Kulen National Park
khmerName: ឧទ្យានជាតិភ្នំគូលែន
description: A sacred mountain plateau considered the birthplace of the Khmer Empire, featuring stunning waterfalls, ancient carvings, and a reclining Buddha.
uniqueFact: The riverbed at Kbal Spean is carved with hundreds of lingas and Hindu iconography — bathing in this water is considered a sacred blessing.
highlights: Two-tiered waterfall perfect for swimming
Kbal Spean river of a thousand lingas
Giant reclining Buddha carved into a cliff
Jungle trekking through protected forest
tip: Go early and on a weekday — it gets very crowded on weekends with local visitors. Bring cash for the entrance fee.

name: Steamed Palm Cake
khmerName: នំស្លាប់ស្រស់
location: Preah Dak Village / Siem Reap Markets
description: A traditional Cambodian street snack made from palm sugar and rice flour, steamed in small clay pots and best eaten warm straight from the vendor.
uniqueFact: Preah Dak village near Angkor is considered the best place in Cambodia to try this cake, made from palms grown on the surrounding floodplain.
highlights: Watch vendors prepare and steam the cakes fresh
Taste the difference between palm sugar and regular sugar
Buy directly from village producers in Preah Dak
Also available at Siem Reap's Old Market
tip: Eat them immediately while warm — they lose their texture within an hour of being made.
openingHours: Early morning – sold out by noon

name: Water Park Khna
khmerName: ឃ្នារ វ៉ាទែរ ផាក
description: A humble, locally loved water park on the outskirts of Siem Reap, with multiple pools, water slides, and a restaurant serving Khmer food — a great way to cool off in the tropical heat.
uniqueFact: This is where local Siem Reap families spend their weekends — visiting gives you a genuine glimpse into everyday Cambodian leisure culture, and you'll likely be the only foreigner there.
highlights: Several open-air water slides for kids and adults
Crystal-clear main pool plus a kiddie pool with enclosed slide
Affordable on-site restaurant serving Khmer dishes, beer, and soft drinks
Entry vouchers usable for food or slide access
tip: Visit on a weekday — Sundays and holidays get very crowded. Bring your own towel, and budget around $3 for entry.

name: Artisans Angkor
khmerName: សិប្បករអង្គរ
description: A social enterprise workshop preserving traditional Khmer craftsmanship by training rural youth in silk weaving, stone carving, lacquerware, and wood carving.
uniqueFact: All artisans are recruited from poor rural communities and receive fair wages, healthcare, and housing — making every purchase a direct investment in Cambodian heritage.
highlights: Free guided workshop tours showing craftspeople at work
Watch silk weaving on traditional looms
Stone and wood carving demonstrations
High quality authentic souvenirs and gifts
tip: The workshop tour is free and fascinating — even if you don't buy anything it's worth the visit.
